summary: Applied ADR 0248 from the latest origin/main-based worktree by replaying the Keycloak, Outline, and public-edge publication paths, wiring the shared logout endpoints through oauth2-proxy and Keycloak, and verifying the final shared-edge plus Outline browser logout journey end to end.

verification.check: Focused repo validation and syntax checks
verification.observed: python3 -m py_compile scripts/session_logout_verify.py passed; uv run --with pyyaml --with pytest python -m pytest tests/test_session_logout_verify.py tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py tests/test_public_edge_oidc_auth_role.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py tests/test_grafana_sso_role.py tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py tests/test_outline_runtime_role.py -q passed with 54 passed in 3.09s; ./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards, ./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-vars, ./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-portals, make syntax-check-keycloak, ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/public-edge.yml --syntax-check, and ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/outline.yml --syntax-check all passed from the isolated worktree.

verification.check: Authoritative service live-apply wrapper on workstream branch
verification.observed: make live-apply-service service=outline env=production reached make check-canonical-truth and stopped because README.md was stale in the isolated worktree. That gate is expected on a non-main workstream branch because README.md is a protected shared integration file. The workstream recorded the direct interface-contract, standby-capacity, redundancy, immutable-replacement, and scoped-playbook evidence instead of refreshing protected mainline truth on the branch.

verification.check: Outline service guardrails for workstream-branch replay
verification.observed: uvx --from pyyaml python scripts/interface_contracts.py --check-live-apply service:outline passed, uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/standby_capacity.py --service outline passed, uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/service_redundancy.py --check-live-apply --service outline reported the declared R0 implemented redundancy state for the current primary-only deployment, and make immutable-guest-replacement-plan service=outline confirmed that this auth/logout change qualified for the documented narrow in-place exception on docker-runtime-lv3.

The first make converge-keycloak env=production run failed transiently while reading the Outline automation user groups from the Keycloak admin API; a direct admin-token API check immediately after returned HTTP 200 for outline.automation with groups grafana-admins and lv3-platform-admins, and the second converge replay completed successfully with docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=153 changed=3 failed=0, monitoring-lv3 : ok=17 changed=0 failed=0, nginx-lv3 : ok=38 changed=4 failed=0, postgres-lv3 : ok=47 changed=0 failed=0, and proxmox_florin : ok=169 changed=0 failed=0.

verification.check: Scoped Outline replay from the isolated worktree
verification.observed: The targeted playbooks/outline.yml replay completed successfully through the ansible_scope_runner path with docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=209 changed=5 failed=0, localhost : ok=25 changed=1 failed=0, nginx-lv3 : ok=38 changed=2 failed=0, and postgres-lv3 : ok=47 changed=0 failed=0, including the repo-managed Outline API token bootstrap and living knowledge sync.

verification.check: Public edge replay and shared logout publication
verification.observed: make configure-edge-publication env=production completed successfully with nginx-lv3 : ok=63 changed=5 failed=0, reloading both oauth2-proxy and NGINX after the shared logout endpoints, Authorization-header extraction, and the wiki.lv3.org CSP override were rendered.

curl -I https://wiki.lv3.org/ returned HTTP 200 with the expected Outline-compatible CSP override; curl -i https://home.lv3.org/.well-known/lv3/session/logout returned HTTP 302 to the shared oauth2-proxy sign-out path; curl -i https://ops.lv3.org/.well-known/lv3/session/proxy-logout returned HTTP 302 to the logged-out landing page; curl -i https://ops.lv3.org/.well-known/lv3/session/logged-out returned HTTP 200 with Cache-Control: no-store; and uv run --with playwright python scripts/session_logout_verify.py --password-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/proxmox_florin_server/.local/keycloak/outline.automation-password.txt passed, verifying both the shared edge logout path and the real Outline UI logout path.

notes: Outline remains the declared product gap for perfect RP-initiated logout because it cannot supply id_token_hint. The live platform now reaches the Keycloak confirmation page consistently, then returns through the shared proxy-cleanup path and the verifier proves both shared-edge and Outline surfaces require a fresh Keycloak login afterward.

The workstream branch intentionally left VERSION, changelog.md, the top-level README.md status summary, and versions/stack.yaml untouched. Those protected shared integration files must wait for the final merge-to-main step.

The verified live platform baseline remained platform version 0.130.45 during this workstream. Do not claim a newer first-live platform version unless a later exact-main replay explicitly updates versions/stack.yaml.
